title: Dynamical Entanglement Phase Transitions in Holographic CFTs
authors: Joseph Dominicus Lap, Jad C. Halimeh, David Horn, Lukas Ebner, Clemens Seidl, Berndt Müller, Andreas Schäfer, Jakob Minar
abstract: We study the time evolution of the entanglement structure of holographic conformal field theories after a local quench. Using the mutual information between two spatial intervals as a probe, we find that $1+1$-dimensional conformal field theories exhibit a rich pattern of dynamical phase transitions. In the large-central-charge limit, mutual information develops sharp non-analyticities at critical times, providing a concrete entanglement-based realization of dynamical quantum phase transitions. We find that the dynamics organize into six distinct phases of mutual information, each controlled by the dominance of a different conformal block, or equivalently, a different holographic geodesic configuration. This phase structure goes beyond the standard quasi-particle picture, explaining non-analytic features that are not captured by simple light-cone propagation from the quench points. We further identify a dynamical $D_4$ symmetry acting on the interval endpoints that controls the presence or absence of mutual information. The onset of mutual information is governed by the breaking of this symmetry to a $\mathbb{Z}_2 \times \mathbb{Z}_2$ subgroup, suggesting a symmetry-based characterization of non-equilibrium entanglement dynamics analogous to the role of symmetry in equilibrium critical phenomena. Finally, numerical studies of critical spin chains indicate that finite-$c$ effects smooth out the sharp large-$c$ transitions between different mutual-information phases, while the transitions between phases with and without mutual information appear to remain non-analytic. These results offer a unifying perspective on real-time entanglement dynamics and their critical features in conformal many-body systems.
summary: This work analyzes dynamical entanglement phase transitions in holographic CFTs following a local quench. By probing the mutual information, the authors find that the dynamics exhibit distinct phases governed by conformal block dominance and a $D_4$ symmetry. This offers a unifying, symmetry-based framework for understanding real-time critical phenomena in many-body systems.
main_problem: The paper investigates whether real-time evolution in non-equilibrium quantum many-body systems exhibits organizing principles analogous to those found in equilibrium statistical mechanics.
main_result: The mutual information in holographic CFTs exhibits a rich pattern of dynamical phase transitions, characterized by sharp non-analyticities in the large-central-charge limit.
method: The study uses the mutual information between spatial intervals as a probe, analyzing the time evolution of entanglement structure following a local quench in holographic CFTs.
model_or_system: The system is a 1+1-dimensional Conformal Field Theory (CFT) with a holographic dual, studied via local quenches. The analysis utilizes the AdS/BCFT correspondence and the Upper Half-Plane (UHP) mapping.
key_observables: Mutual Information ($I_{A:B}$), Entanglement Entropy ($S_A$), and the time evolution of these quantities.
important_parameters: Time ($t$), Central Charge ($c$), and the symmetry group $D_4$.
main_assumptions: The analysis is performed in the large-central-charge limit ($c 	o \infty$), and the holographic entanglement entropy is assumed via the Ryu-Takayanagi conjecture.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the phase structure of entanglement entropy on the UHP based on cross-ratios, and show non-analytic peaks in the entanglement difference ($\Delta S$) during quench protocols.
paper_structure: The paper progresses from establishing the static phase structure of entanglement on the UHP, relating this to quench dynamics, and finally identifying the symmetry ($D_4$) governing the onset and nature of the dynamical phase transitions.
why_interesting: It provides a concrete, entanglement-based realization of dynamical quantum phase transitions, linking non-equilibrium dynamics to deep symmetries and geometric structures derived from holography.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. The expansion of a 4-point function in the s- and t-channels.

FIG. 2. A sketch of the extremal geodesics in the presence of a boundary Q (red). In addition to the connected geodesic Econ X (solid black), one also has to take into account the discon- nected geodesic Edis X (dashed black) that ends on the boundary Q, viz. the end-of-the-world brane.

FIG. 3. First Row: The world-sheets for a) splitting quench, b) joining quench c) splitting quench at finite temperature d) joining quench at finite temperature. Second Row: The corresponding world-sheets at t = 0.
FIG. 4. Disjoint subsystem X ∪Y where the subsystems have a cross-ratio |x| < 1/2 (left) and |x| > 1/2 (right).

FIG. 5. Upper half-plane with two subsystems X = [z1, z2] and Y = [z3, z4] and their mirror images ¯ X = [¯z2, ¯z2] and ¯Y = [¯z3, ¯z4] on the lower half-plane. (Red: Conformal boundary separating upper and lower half-plane).
